Implemented MountRuntime API, typed modes, status/diff/snapshot runtime methods, and runtime tests.
Materialized mount is implemented as a MountRuntime adapter and covered by runtime status/unmount/recover tests.
MountInfo now records selected mode, state, upper/lower/work paths, PID, and timestamps in mount metadata.
Auto mode now selects overlayfs, then fuse-overlayfs, then materialized; explicit unsupported modes return prerequisite errors.
MountRuntime API, materialized adapter, selected mode metadata, and mode selection policy are implemented and tested.
Overlay/FUSE runtime prepares a restored lowerdir chain plus upper and work dirs under .osix/mounts/<id>/ with private permissions.
Linux overlay mode mounts kernel overlayfs with lowerdir, upperdir, and workdir and unmounts through the runtime interface.
Snapshot detects overlay/FUSE mount metadata, packages upperdir entries, and preserves overlay whiteouts; covered by unit tests.
Added Linux-only overlay integration test with prerequisite skips; verified linux test binary cross-compiles.
Linux overlayfs runtime, upperdir snapshotting, platform checks, and integration tests are implemented.
Documented and implemented the first FUSE backend as a fuse-overlayfs platform adapter behind MountRuntime, with build-tag handling and prerequisite checks.
FUSE mode starts fuse-overlayfs over restored lowerdir plus upperdir; Linux integration test verifies mounted reads when prerequisites exist.
FUSE copy-on-write mutations are delegated to fuse-overlayfs upperdir semantics; integration test covers write/create/delete through the mounted tree.
Dirty index is rebuilt from FUSE/overlay upperdir state, including whiteout deletions, and watch consumes upperdir dirty bytes.
Snapshot packages FUSE upperdir entries and whiteouts, with unit and Linux integration coverage for snapshot/restore equivalence.
Added Linux-only fuse-overlayfs integration test with clear prerequisite skips and mount/write/snapshot/restore/unmount coverage.
Rootless FUSE mode is implemented through the fuse-overlayfs adapter with dirty tracking, snapshotting, docs, and Linux integration tests.
Watch now uses runtime upperdir dirty bytes for overlay/FUSE mounts and falls back to tree scans for materialized targets.
Watch calls the runtime flush hook before creating snapshots, rebuilding dirty state and validating runtime permissions for overlay/FUSE mounts.
Watch integration consumes runtime dirty bytes and flushes runtime dirty state before snapshots.
Mount status reports selected mode/state and marks stale mounts when runtime PID or mount presence is invalid.
osix mount recover reloads metadata, validates runtime dirs, rebuilds dirty.json, and preserves upperdir content.
Runtime cache is created private and world-writable cache/upper/work/lower dirs are rejected; permission failures are tested.
Overlay/FUSE availability checks return clear prerequisite errors and platform tests skip with those reasons when unavailable.
Documented runtime modes, prerequisites, state layout, dirty tracking, recovery, security checks, and FUSE adapter decision.
Recovery, status/stale detection, permission hardening, platform checks, and runtime docs are implemented and tested.
Completed MountRuntime, overlayfs, fuse-overlayfs adapter, watch integration, recovery/hardening, docs, and verification for the filesystem runtime scope.
Selected macFUSE union backend using unionfs/unionfs-fuse and recorded decision in docs/decisions/0003-macos-overlay-backend.md.
Updated FUSE/overlay runtime spec with macOS overlay-style semantics, mode mapping, prerequisites, and platform differences.
Added Darwin prerequisite checks for macFUSE plus unionfs/unionfs-fuse and tests for explicit errors and auto materialized fallback.
Documented macOS prerequisites, mode behavior, smoke harness, setup requirements, and limitations in runtime filesystem docs.
Added scripts/test-macos-overlay.sh and scripts/build-macos-unionfs.sh; smoke harness checks prerequisites, uses .osix-tools/bin, and runs Darwin integration when macFUSE is loadable.

Replaced Darwin macFUSE/unionfs runtime path with FSKit helper boundary, added osix-fskitctl Swift helper scaffold, updated tests for FSKit prerequisites/materialized fallback, and documented ADR 0004.

Implemented explicit osix push/osix pull CLI commands, default registry transport selection, docs/spec updates, and CLI fake-registry test coverage. Verified go test ./..., go test ./cmd/osix -count=1 -v, and git diff --check.

Implemented OSIx signature/provenance registry artifacts as subject-bearing OCI image manifests with deterministic fallback tags. Push publishes local signature/provenance blobs and artifact manifests; pull imports them through Referrers API discovery or fallback tags; VerifySnapshot works after pull. Verified TestPushPullSignedSnapshotReferrersThroughOCIRegistry, go test ./..., scripts/test-registry-docker.sh, and git diff --check.

Added scripts/test-registry-hosted.sh and verified hosted registry compatibility against GHCR at ghcr.io/smol-platform/smol-agent-oci-fs-compat. The harness pushes a signed snapshot with a unique tag, pulls it into a fresh workspace, verifies signature/provenance after pull, restores, and checks content. Docker Distribution and GHCR are now documented in docs/compatibility-matrix.md.

Implemented OSIx registry credential discovery and Bearer challenge handling, then verified authenticated hosted push/pull against GHCR with scripts/test-registry-hosted.sh. The run pushed a signed snapshot to ghcr.io/smol-platform/smol-agent-oci-fs-compat, pulled it into a fresh workspace, verified signature/provenance, restored, and checked content. Credentials remain outside manifests, refs, logs, and blobs.

Completed OCI repository pull/push pivot: explicit osix push/pull commands, digest-verified blob/config/manifest transfer, authenticated Basic/Bearer/Docker-config registry requests, OSIx signature/provenance artifact push/pull via referrers with fallback tags, Docker Distribution compatibility harness, and GHCR hosted compatibility harness. Verified go test ./..., scripts/test-registry-docker.sh, OSIX_HOSTED_REGISTRY_REPO=ghcr.io/smol-platform/smol-agent-oci-fs-compat scripts/test-registry-hosted.sh, shell syntax checks, and git diff --check.
Implemented in this checkpoint: OSIx layer envelope, mixed-recipient restore coverage, and documentation for recipient syntax and prototype limits.
Implemented in this checkpoint: OSIx layer envelope, mixed-recipient restore coverage, and documentation for recipient syntax and prototype limits.
Implemented in this checkpoint: OSIx layer envelope, mixed-recipient restore coverage, and documentation for recipient syntax and prototype limits.

Implemented provider-backed key wrapping: AWS KMS through the AWS CLI or command hooks, GPG through the gpg command, and endpoint recipients through an HTTP JSON wrap/unwrap protocol. Added provider tests covering AWS CLI shape, KMS command hooks, GPG command invocation, endpoint fixture wrapping, single-KMS provider envelopes, and mixed restore. Updated docs and security spec.

Implemented provider-backed key wrapping: AWS KMS through the AWS CLI or command hooks, GPG through the gpg command, and endpoint recipients through an HTTP JSON wrap/unwrap protocol. Added provider tests covering AWS CLI shape, KMS command hooks, GPG command invocation, endpoint fixture wrapping, single-KMS provider envelopes, and mixed restore. Updated docs and security spec.

Implemented provider-backed key wrapping: AWS KMS through the AWS CLI or command hooks, GPG through the gpg command, and endpoint recipients through an HTTP JSON wrap/unwrap protocol. Added provider tests covering AWS CLI shape, KMS command hooks, GPG command invocation, endpoint fixture wrapping, single-KMS provider envelopes, and mixed restore. Updated docs and security spec.

Completed multi-type encryption support: mixed OSIx layer envelopes, age, KMS, GPG, and endpoint recipients, provider-backed KMS/GPG/endpoint modes, docs, specs, and automated verification with go test ./... and git diff --check.
Implemented cosign simple-signing payload creation, ECDSA P-256 signature metadata, sha256-<subject>.sig publication, pull import, and cryptographic test coverage.
Implemented Sigstore v0.3 message-signature and DSSE attestation bundle blobs, subject-bearing bundle manifests, sha256-<subject> referrers-tag fallback index, pull import, docs, and tests.
Implemented trusted ECDSA P-256 PEM key parsing and cosign simple-signing verification while preserving ed25519 OSIx verification; covered correct and wrong-key paths in go test ./....

Implemented Sigstore bundle policy verification with sigstore-go: verify/restore flags enforce certificate identity, OIDC issuer, trusted root/TUF, Rekor tlog, observer timestamp, and Fulcio SCT checks. Added public-good bundle coverage and local bundle parse coverage; verified go test ./... and scripts/test-registry-docker.sh.

Implemented opt-in public Sigstore keyless signing via --sign sigstore-keyless. Snapshot signing now accepts OIDC identity tokens from flag/file/env, resolves Fulcio/Rekor/TUF/signing-config inputs, emits cosign simple-signing metadata and certificate-backed Sigstore v0.3 signature/attestation bundles, and preserves local keyless signing as keyless/keyless-local. Added fixture-style certificate-backed signing verification through VerifySnapshot; verified go test ./..., scripts/test-registry-docker.sh, and git diff --check.

Completed full Sigstore/cosign compatibility scope: signed snapshots publish/pull cosign simple-signing and Sigstore bundle artifacts in OCI-compatible locations; trusted ECDSA key verification validates cosign artifacts; Sigstore certificate policy verification enforces identity, issuer, trusted root/TUF, Rekor tlog, observer timestamp, and SCT checks; --sign sigstore-keyless creates certificate-backed public Sigstore bundles from configured OIDC/Fulcio/Rekor/TUF inputs while preserving local keyless signing. Verified with go test ./..., scripts/test-registry-docker.sh, and git diff --check.
Implemented GitHub issue reference adapter and documented adapter contract
Remote expected-parent conflict handling, tests, distinct exit code, and provider CAS limitation docs are complete
Persistent watch daemon lifecycle, list/status/stop/restart, stale recovery, and surfaced push failures are implemented and tested

Implemented and verified CLI helper-process lazy FUSE remote snapshot e2e. Added Linux-only cmd/osix test that builds a real osix binary, uses a fake OCI registry, mounts --mode fuse --lazy --rw=false, verifies external helper PID/state, proves no layer fetch before file read, reads through FUSE, confirms one lazy layer fetch, keeps lowerdir unmaterialized, and unmounts the helper. scripts/test-fuse-docker.sh now runs both internal/osix and cmd/osix FUSE runtime integration tests; Docker run passed.

Implemented writable Linux native lazy FUSE copy-up. --mode fuse --lazy now uses the Go FUSE lower-store backend for read-only and writable mounts, skips lowerdir Restore, serves lower metadata/content lazily, copies lower files into upperdir on write, writes new upper files, emits .wh.* whiteouts for lower deletes, and preserves Diff/Snapshot/Restore through the existing upperdir pipeline. Docker FUSE integration now covers in-process writable lazy FUSE and CLI helper writable remote lazy FUSE with no layer fetch before copy-up, one layer fetch on copy-up, upperdir file/whiteout verification, snapshot, restore, and clean helper unmount.

Implemented lazy remote reads and lazy runtime lower read-through for the supported runtime paths. pull --lazy/read cover on-demand unencrypted reads, cache hits, missing blob failures, and restore fallback. Darwin FSKit and Linux native FUSE mounts now skip lowerdir Restore and service lower metadata/content lazily; Linux native FUSE supports writable copy-up, new upper files, .wh.* whiteouts, Diff/Snapshot/Restore, and helper-process CLI remote mounts. --mode auto --lazy selects native FUSE before kernel overlay, and explicit --mode overlay --lazy is rejected instead of silently materializing the lowerdir. Docker FUSE integration passed for read-only, writable, auto-routing, overlay rejection, and CLI helper remote mounts.

Implemented and verified encrypted writable Linux lazy FUSE lowerdirs. selectMountMode now routes all --lazy FUSE mounts through native lazy FUSE availability and auto lazy no longer falls through to kernel overlay. Added TestLazyFUSEWritableEncryptedRuntimeIntegrationLinux, which deletes the whole encrypted layer, proves wrong decrypt material fails without restoring it, then reads and copy-ups plaintext upper content with valid decrypt material without restoring the whole layer. Verified scripts/test-fuse-docker.sh, go test ./..., bash -n scripts, and git diff --check.

Completed encrypted lazy random access across read, range read, registry lazy pull, restore, Darwin FSKit lower reads, and Linux native lazy FUSE lower reads/copy-up. Encrypted snapshots emit encrypted per-file blobs, chunk descriptors, Merkle metadata, and plaintext integrity metadata for age-only, legacy KMS, and OSIx-envelope modes. Restore --decrypt now uses encrypted lazy indexes to materialize files from lazy blobs without fetching whole encrypted layers when indexes are available, including remote lazy-pulled refs; wrong decrypt material fails before per-file fetch. Coverage includes tampered file/chunk/plaintext metadata rejection, wrong identities, cache reuse, whole-layer compatibility fallback, remote lazy range fetches, Linux encrypted writable FUSE copy-up, and local/remote encrypted lazy restore. Verified go test ./..., focused restore/registry tests, bash -n scripts, and git diff --check.

Implemented watch retention policy flags/state, watch-triggered compaction, checkpoint push with expected-parent protection, and local/remote pruning. Verified with go test ./... and live Docker integration ./scripts/test-retention-docker.sh, which pushed watch changes, checkpointed, pruned local blobs/refs and remote manifests, and restored remote:main from the checkpoint.

Retention milestone complete. Watch can snapshot/push changed Linux agent state, automatically compact chains into checkpoint snapshots by policy, push checkpoint branch/latest tags, prune local refs/blobs, opt-in prune remote manifests, and pass the live Docker registry integration script.

Leaf work verified independently: automatic snapshots apply encryption/signing policy, reporter/docs cover no-secret-leak behavior, and README/operator docs describe automatic CSI snapshotting and the Docker E2E. Prerequisite registry secret projection, Kind/gtr, and release tasks remain open.

Added Prometheus-format CSI counters for publish, unpublish, snapshot, snapshot errors, and checkpoints; exposed /metrics from serve and a metrics listener for serve-csi; verified by metrics tests and go test ./....

All status/reporting scope is implemented: snapshot CR create/patch, filesystem status patching, Events/logging, Kubernetes reporter hardening, and automatic snapshot metrics. Cluster E2E/release tasks remain tracked separately under M12.5.

Implemented scheme-aware registry repo/reference parsing and client base URL selection, preserved explicit schemes in lazy remote blob metadata, updated Kind harness to use http:// stateRef/sourceRef, and verified with go test ./..., scripts/test-k8s-autosnap-docker.sh, and scripts/test-k8s-autosnap-kind.sh.

scripts/test-k8s-autosnap-kind.sh now creates a local registry, deploys operator/CSI with registrar and CSIDriver, creates filesystem/policy CRs plus CSI PV/PVCs, runs a writer pod without osix watch, waits for automatic snapshot push via AgentOCIFileSystem status, restores a reader pod from stateRef:main, and passed end to end.

Restore verification now runs before CSI mount handoff whenever signing policy is configured. trustedKeySecretRef is read from Kubernetes Secret data and mapped to OSIx VerifyOptions; Sigstore certificate identity/issuer/trusted-root policy fields are exposed in AgentOCIFileSystem, CRD schema, CSI volume context, and docs, then mapped to VerifyOptions. Tests cover unsigned restore rejection, signed restore acceptance, Sigstore policy mapping, CSI context parsing, and go test ./....

Implemented registrySecretRef resolution through node SecretProvider, in-cluster Kubernetes Secret reads with bearer token/CA, temporary env/DOCKER_CONFIG projection around pull/push only, env restoration and cleanup, and mount-record no-secret-leak coverage. Verified by TestRegistryCredentialsProjectedTemporarily, TestKubernetesSecretProviderDecodesSecretData, go test ./..., Docker E2E, and Kind E2E.

All registry credential, signing/encryption, no-secret-leak, and restore verification children are closed. Current evidence includes registrySecretRef projection tests, Kubernetes Secret provider tests, Sigstore/trusted-key VerifyOptions mapping, source verification tests, go test ./..., Docker autosnapshot E2E, and Kind autosnapshot/restore E2E.

Published versioned linux/amd64 images to GHCR and pushed release tag v0.1.1. Verified remote digests: operator sha256:df5a925a1610058148046f3a062cd6f7a1d78759aa48bfe96040910cb4e13092 and CSI sha256:113985cfdb1a56d9831d79063a60bc34b38d840acc1d7c73653b5ec8d3f08c6d. README/docs now use scripts/release-k8s-images.sh with v0.1.1 and show GHCR pull-secret/live verification flow. Release tag message records go test ./..., Docker autosnapshot E2E, Kind autosnapshot E2E, and image digests. m5.2 live gtr verification remains open separately.

gtr live-cluster verification is implemented and passed. The script supports explicit kube context, private GHCR image pull and registry push credentials, k0s kubelet root patching, controlled post-first-snapshot mutation, second digest detection, checkpoint detection, reader restore from stateRef:main, and bounded cleanup of test pods/PVCs/PVs/namespaces. Final evidence: snapshot sha256:cd41b912077cd76dd92fc11ac50d2045172732079aec8dc29c25f72058195f5b, checkpoint sha256:c242bdd6a55038ef7d03d3b20f2b6fb05b007443c3c206c13d2dfde7fbf3fba6, stateRef ghcr.io/smol-platform/smol-agent-oci-fs-autosnap-live; no temporary osix-autosnap namespaces or test PVs remained after cleanup.

All M12.5 cluster E2E, manifest, release, and documentation children are closed. Evidence includes Docker autosnapshot E2E, Kind autosnapshot/restore E2E, published v0.1.1 operator/CSI images, updated docs/release notes, and live gtr E2E proving writer mutation, two automatic snapshot pushes, checkpoint, AgentOCISnapshot status, reader restore from stateRef:main, and cleanup.

CSI-native automatic snapshotting is complete for the M12 implementation milestone. Evidence: go test ./... passed; scripts/test-k8s-autosnap-docker.sh and scripts/test-k8s-autosnap-kind.sh previously passed; v0.1.1 images were published; live gtr run passed with snapshot sha256:cd41b912077cd76dd92fc11ac50d2045172732079aec8dc29c25f72058195f5b and checkpoint sha256:c242bdd6a55038ef7d03d3b20f2b6fb05b007443c3c206c13d2dfde7fbf3fba6; the reader workload restored gtr-v2/notes/second marker from stateRef:main; temporary namespaces/PVs were cleaned. Future roadmap expansion remains tracked under osix-k8s-operator-roadmap, not as unfinished M12 autosnapshot scope.
Implemented containment-safe collision-resistant CSI workspace mapping shared by planner/runtime, with traversal, separator, Unicode, empty, and collision tests.
Implemented request-target fallback unmount and record error handling; verified missing, corrupt, stale, already-unmounted, materialized, overlay, and FUSE-capable paths.
Implemented worker completion tracking, context-bounded stop/join, per-volume snapshot serialization, and unpublish ordering with deterministic blocked-worker and replacement tests.
Propagated and persisted CSI read-only access, enforced read-only materialized/overlay/FUSE behavior, rejected empty read-only volumes, and verified retry semantics.

Implemented immutable workspace identity validation, atomic mutable config writes with publish rollback, restart reconciliation, and republish tests across repository, branch, base, name, mount, and encryption.
Moved signing before publication and added transactional multi-ref updates with rollback; verified signer, attestation, expected-parent, and injected rename failures leave refs unchanged.
Implemented same-filesystem staged restore, replay/tree verification, rollback-safe target replacement and backup recovery, stale-stage cleanup, and fetch/decrypt/extract/final-rename failure tests.
Made deploy/kubernetes canonical, generated the embedded render stream, added CI/unit drift checks and documentation, and verified normalized equality with kubectl kustomize.
